summary: Antiepileptic drugs have been linked to an elevated risk of suicide prior to the FDA warning, but the agency's caution prompted enhanced clinical vigilance, leading to more rigorous screening, patient counseling, and monitoring during prescriber decisions—indicating observable changes in prescribing practice after the warning was issued. The FDA warning did not introduce new risks; instead, it amplified awareness and standardization of safety measures already present in some guidelines.
